proposed_hypotheses.text: Among the 50000 patients in ds001_aml, the marginal probability of objective_response is below 25% (i.e. responders are the minority class).

analyses.code: df['objective_response'].value_counts(normalize=True)
analyses.result_summary: Overall objective_response rate is 8452/50000 = 16.9%; non-response is the dominant class. The 16.9% baseline is consistent with a hard-to-treat oncology setting and motivates analysing predictors as deviations from this base rate.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Across all 80 binary features, at least one binary feature is associated with objective_response (response rate differs between feature=1 and feature=0).
Patients with feature_035=1 have a HIGHER objective_response rate than patients with feature_035=0.
Patients with feature_093=1 have a HIGHER objective_response rate than patients with feature_093=0.
Patients with feature_121=1 have a LOWER objective_response rate than patients with feature_121=0.

analyses.code: for c in binary_features: chi2_contingency(pd.crosstab(df[c], df['objective_response']))
analyses.result_summary: Chi-square screen of all 80 binary features: feature_035 is by far the strongest, with response 22.13% (n=3624) when feature_035=1 vs 16.50% (n=46376) when feature_035=0 — an absolute difference of +5.63 percentage points (chi-square p=3.5e-18).

analyses.code: chi2_contingency(pd.crosstab(df['feature_093'], df['objective_response']))
analyses.result_summary: feature_093=1: response 18.48% (n=4025) vs feature_093=0: 16.77% (n=45975); absolute difference +1.72 pp, chi-square p=0.0056.

analyses.code: chi2_contingency(pd.crosstab(df['feature_121'], df['objective_response']))
analyses.result_summary: feature_121=1: response 15.64% (n=4143) vs feature_121=0: 17.02% (n=45857); absolute difference -1.38 pp, chi-square p=0.025. Direction matches hypothesis (lower).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among the 36 continuous features, at least one differs in mean between responders and non-responders.
Mean feature_011 is LOWER in responders than in non-responders.
Mean feature_006 is LOWER in responders than in non-responders.
Mean feature_099 is HIGHER in responders than in non-responders.
Mean feature_092 is LOWER in responders than in non-responders.

analyses.code: ttest_ind(df.loc[y==1,'feature_011'], df.loc[y==0,'feature_011'], equal_var=False)
analyses.result_summary: feature_011: mean in responders 3.38 vs non-responders 3.95 (difference -0.57, Welch t-test p=7.2e-39). The strongest continuous predictor in the screen.

analyses.code: ttest_ind(df.loc[y==1,'feature_006'], df.loc[y==0,'feature_006'], equal_var=False)
analyses.result_summary: feature_006: mean in responders 58.23 vs non-responders 60.31 (difference -2.08, p=9.5e-20).

analyses.code: ttest_ind(df.loc[y==1,'feature_099'], df.loc[y==0,'feature_099'], equal_var=False)
analyses.result_summary: feature_099: mean in responders 3.825 vs non-responders 3.796 (difference +0.029, p=1.3e-6). Direction matches hypothesis (higher).

analyses.code: ttest_ind(df.loc[y==1,'feature_092'], df.loc[y==0,'feature_092'], equal_var=False)
analyses.result_summary: feature_092: mean in responders 19.07 vs non-responders 20.40 (difference -1.33, p=1.3e-5). Direction matches hypothesis.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Objective_response rate differs across the five categories of feature_005 (race: white / black / hispanic / asian / other).
Objective_response rate differs across the four categories of feature_087 (insurance: medicare / private / medicaid / uninsured).

analyses.code: chi2_contingency(pd.crosstab(df['feature_005'], df['objective_response']))
analyses.result_summary: Response rates by race: white 17.28% (n=32498), hispanic 16.70% (n=7631), asian 16.33% (n=2970), black 15.74% (n=5889), other 14.72% (n=1012). Chi-square p=0.0096 — there IS a small overall race difference; white patients have the highest crude rate, 'other' the lowest. Maximum pairwise gap ~2.6 pp.

analyses.code: chi2_contingency(pd.crosstab(df['feature_087'], df['objective_response']))
analyses.result_summary: Response rates by insurance: medicare 17.18%, medicaid 16.87%, private 16.66%, uninsured 16.62%. Range only 0.6 pp. Chi-square p=0.54 — no detectable insurance disparity in the crude analysis.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher values of the ordinal feature_057 (range 0-2) are associated with LOWER objective_response (suggesting feature_057 captures something like worsening performance status / disease burden).

Among the other ordinal-integer features (feature_096, _064, _018, _045, _042, _125), at least one is significantly associated with response (testing whether the ordinal screen turns up additional staging-like covariates).

analyses.code: spearmanr(df['feature_057'], df['objective_response']); chi2_contingency(crosstab)
analyses.result_summary: feature_057 levels 0/1/2 have response rates of 21.29% (n=17592), 15.52% (n=24971), and 11.19% (n=7437). Monotonic decrease. Chi-square p=5.5e-98; Spearman rho=-0.0946 (p=7.7e-100). This is the single strongest predictor of objective_response in the dataset.

analyses.code: spearmanr for each of feature_096, feature_064, feature_018, feature_045, feature_042, feature_125
analyses.result_summary: None of feature_096, feature_064, feature_018, feature_045, or feature_042 reaches p<0.05 (all chi-square p>=0.09). feature_125 is weakly negatively associated (Spearman rho=-0.0104, p=0.020) but the rate gradient (17.4% -> 15.7% across levels 0->4) is small and not robust to multiple comparisons.

proposed_hypotheses.text: In a multivariable logistic regression for objective_response that includes feature_057, feature_035, feature_011, feature_006, feature_063, feature_099, feature_092, feature_093, feature_121, feature_014, feature_078 (a candidate age proxy), race (feature_005), and insurance (feature_087), each of feature_057, feature_035, feature_011, feature_006, feature_063, feature_099, and feature_092 retains an INDEPENDENT and directionally consistent association with response.
After adjustment for the clinical covariates above, the categorical race effect (feature_005) is NO LONGER significant — i.e., the crude race difference seen in iteration 4 is largely confounded.

analyses.code: smf.logit('objective_response ~ feature_057 + feature_035 + feature_011 + feature_006 + feature_063 + feature_099 + feature_092 + feature_093 + feature_121 + feature_014 + feature_078 + C(feature_005) + C(feature_087)', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Multivariable logit (n=50000, pseudo-R^2=0.019, LLR p=8e-172): feature_057 beta=-0.384 (OR 0.68, p<0.001); feature_035 beta=+0.361 (OR 1.43, p<0.001); feature_011 beta=-0.040 (p<0.001); feature_006 beta=-0.0057 (p<0.001); feature_063 beta=-0.0072 (p<0.001); feature_099 beta=+0.118 (p<0.001); feature_092 beta=-0.0022 (p<0.001); feature_093 beta=+0.126 (p=0.003); feature_121 beta=-0.088 (p=0.050); feature_014 beta=+0.059 (p=0.017). All directions match the univariate findings. feature_078 (age proxy) beta=+0.0003 (p=0.76) — NOT significant. Every race indicator (vs reference) has p>=0.12; every insurance indicator p>=0.55. Hypothesis 15 SUPPORTED; hypothesis 16 SUPPORTED.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The favorable effect of feature_035 on objective_response is MAGNIFIED at higher values of feature_057 — i.e., feature_035 'rescues' high-risk patients more than low-risk patients (positive interaction term).

analyses.code: smf.logit('objective_response ~ feature_035 * feature_057 + feature_011 + feature_006', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: feature_035:feature_057 interaction coef = +0.034 (p=0.59). The interaction is NOT significant; main effects are large and consistent. Hypothesis 17 REFUTED — feature_035 acts roughly additively on the log-odds scale across feature_057 strata, not multiplicatively.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Even though the interaction term is not significant, the direction of the feature_035 effect (positive on response) is preserved within EACH level of feature_057, supporting a robust main effect.

analyses.code: for level in [0,1,2]: chi2 within df[df['feature_057']==level]
analyses.result_summary: Stratified by feature_057: at level 0, response is 27.0% with feature_035=1 vs 20.8% without (delta +6.1 pp, p=1.7e-7); at level 1, 20.5% vs 15.1% (delta +5.3 pp, p=2.5e-9); at level 2, 15.3% vs 10.9% (delta +4.4 pp, p=0.003). The effect is positive, significant, and of similar magnitude in all three strata.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The continuous feature_011 has a stronger negative association with response at higher levels of feature_057 (feature_011 × feature_057 interaction).

analyses.code: smf.logit('objective_response ~ feature_011 * feature_057 + feature_035 + feature_006', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: feature_011:feature_057 interaction coef = -0.0023 (p=0.65). NOT significant; feature_011 acts additively across feature_057 strata. Hypothesis 19 REFUTED.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The negative association of feature_006 with response is attenuated (less negative) in feature_035=1 patients vs feature_035=0 (positive feature_006 × feature_035 interaction).

analyses.code: smf.logit('objective_response ~ feature_006 * feature_035 + feature_057', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: feature_006:feature_035 interaction coef = +0.0022 (p=0.31). NOT significant. Both features contribute additively. Hypothesis 20 REFUTED.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_011 and feature_035 interact: the negative effect of feature_011 is amplified in the feature_035=1 subset (negative interaction term).

analyses.code: smf.logit('objective_response ~ feature_011 * feature_035 + feature_057', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: feature_011:feature_035 interaction coef = -0.0135 (p=0.25). NOT significant. Hypothesis 21 REFUTED.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The favorable effect of feature_035 on objective_response differs across racial groups (feature_035 × feature_005 interaction).

analyses.code: LR test of feature_035 * C(feature_005) vs feature_035 + C(feature_005), both with feature_057
analyses.result_summary: Likelihood-ratio test for feature_035 × race interaction: chi2=2.28 on 4 df, p=0.68. NOT significant. Stratum-specific feature_035 effects: white +6.5 pp, asian +4.0 pp, black +4.1 pp, hispanic +4.1 pp, other +5.4 pp — all positive, all of comparable magnitude. Hypothesis 22 REFUTED — feature_035 benefit appears RACIALLY EQUITABLE.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The (negative) prognostic effect of feature_011 differs across racial groups (feature_011 × feature_005 interaction).

analyses.code: LR test of feature_011 * C(feature_005) vs feature_011 + C(feature_005), both with feature_057 + feature_035
analyses.result_summary: Likelihood-ratio test: p=0.91. NOT significant. The continuous feature_011 effect is consistent across races. Hypothesis 23 REFUTED.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The (negative) effect of feature_006 on response differs across racial groups (feature_006 × feature_005 interaction).

analyses.code: LR test of feature_006 * C(feature_005) vs feature_006 + C(feature_005), both with feature_057 + feature_035
analyses.result_summary: Likelihood-ratio test: p=0.36. NOT significant. Hypothesis 24 REFUTED. Together with hypotheses 22 and 23, the dominant predictors act consistently across races.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The favorable effect of feature_035 on response differs across insurance categories (feature_035 × feature_087 interaction).

analyses.code: LR test of feature_035 * C(feature_087) vs feature_035 + C(feature_087)
analyses.result_summary: Likelihood-ratio test: p=0.091. Borderline but NOT significant at alpha=0.05. Some suggestive heterogeneity by insurance, but no firm evidence. Hypothesis 25 mostly REFUTED.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The negative prognostic effect of feature_057 (PS-like) differs by insurance category, e.g. high-feature_057 patients fare worse if uninsured.

analyses.code: LR test of feature_057 * C(feature_087) vs feature_057 + C(feature_087)
analyses.result_summary: Likelihood-ratio test: p=0.96. NOT significant. The dose-response of feature_057 is essentially identical across insurance categories. Hypothesis 26 REFUTED.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Adjusting only for feature_057 (the strongest single covariate) is sufficient to make the race main effect non-significant — i.e. most of the crude race-response association from iteration 4 is mediated/confounded through feature_057.

analyses.code: smf.logit('objective_response ~ C(feature_005) + feature_057', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: After adjusting only for feature_057, all race indicator p-values >= 0.12 (white vs reference 'asian': beta +0.074, p=0.16; black: beta -0.039, p=0.53; hispanic: beta +0.032, p=0.59; other: beta -0.120, p=0.24). However, the distribution of feature_057 across races is nearly IDENTICAL (level 0/1/2 fractions ~35% / 50% / 15% in every race), so feature_057 cannot mediate the crude race effect — it is more likely that other clinical covariates jointly account for the small residual. The significant crude race chi-square (p=0.0096) was small in magnitude and is not robust to adjustment. Hypothesis 27 PARTIALLY SUPPORTED in outcome (race becomes NS) but the mediating mechanism is NOT feature_057 specifically.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_078 (range 18-92, mean 68 — likely an age proxy) has an INDEPENDENT NEGATIVE association with objective_response after adjusting for feature_057, feature_035, feature_011, and feature_006.

analyses.code: smf.logit('objective_response ~ feature_078 + feature_057 + feature_035 + feature_011 + feature_006', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: feature_078 adjusted beta = +0.0003 per unit (p=0.75). NOT significant; the point estimate is essentially zero. Crude univariate test (responders vs non) was also NS (means 67.99 vs 67.99). Hypothesis 28 REFUTED — feature_078 (presumed age) does not predict response after adjustment, suggesting either that age is not a strong predictor in this AML cohort or that feature_057 already absorbs age-related risk.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Older patients (high feature_078) lose more response benefit per unit of feature_057 — i.e. negative feature_078 × feature_057 interaction.

analyses.code: smf.logit('objective_response ~ feature_078 * feature_057 + feature_035 + feature_011', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Interaction coef = +0.0020 per (year × level), p=0.23. NOT significant; sign is opposite to hypothesis. Hypothesis 29 REFUTED.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among the highest-risk subgroup (feature_057=2), the favorable effect of feature_035 on response remains significant and sizable (beta > 0, p<0.05), suggesting it could represent a treatment whose benefit persists even in the most fragile patients.

analyses.code: smf.logit on subset df[df['feature_057']==2]
analyses.result_summary: Within feature_057=2 (n=7437): feature_035 beta=+0.393 (OR 1.48, p=0.002); feature_011 beta=-0.053 (p<0.001); feature_006 beta=-0.0082 (p<0.001); feature_063 and feature_099 NS in this stratum. Race and insurance NS. Hypothesis 30 SUPPORTED — feature_035 effect is preserved (and numerically larger) in the worst-prognosis stratum.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the lowest-risk subgroup (feature_057=0), the same primary predictors (feature_035, feature_011, feature_006, feature_099) remain independently associated with response in the same directions as the overall model.

analyses.code: smf.logit on subset df[df['feature_057']==0]
analyses.result_summary: Within feature_057=0 (n=17592): feature_035 beta=+0.337 (p=2e-7); feature_011 beta=-0.041 (p<0.001); feature_006 beta=-0.0052 (p<0.001); feature_099 beta=+0.118 (p=0.001); feature_063 borderline (p=0.087). All directions match the overall model. Race is NS (chi2 p=0.138 for the crude race contrast even in this most-favorable stratum). Hypothesis 31 SUPPORTED.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After adjustment for the strong primary predictors, feature_093 (positive direction) and feature_121 (negative direction) remain independently associated with response, while several other binary 'screen hits' (feature_111, feature_030, feature_091, feature_053) lose significance.

analyses.code: smf.logit with feature_035, feature_093, feature_121, feature_111, feature_030, feature_091, feature_053, feature_057, feature_011, feature_006
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted estimates: feature_093 beta=+0.126 (p=0.003) — robust positive predictor; feature_121 beta=-0.091 (p=0.043) — significant negative predictor; feature_111 beta=-0.080 (p=0.051) — borderline; feature_030 beta=+0.102 (p=0.058) — borderline; feature_091 beta=-0.220 (p=0.094) — NS; feature_053 beta=-0.184 (p=0.153) — NS. Hypothesis 32 SUPPORTED for feature_093 and feature_121; the smaller-prevalence binary signals (feature_091, feature_053) do not survive adjustment.

proposed_hypotheses.text: A composite multivariable logistic model that combines feature_057, feature_035, feature_011, feature_006, feature_063, feature_099, feature_092, feature_093, feature_121, feature_014, feature_078, race, and insurance achieves discrimination above chance (AUC > 0.55) but well below clinically excellent (AUC < 0.7), reflecting the modest pseudo-R^2 (~0.02).

analyses.code: compute concordance C-statistic from predicted probabilities of the iteration-6 model
analyses.result_summary: Apparent (in-sample) AUC of the composite model = 0.598. Above chance (0.5) but modest. Hypothesis 33 SUPPORTED. Discrimination is dominated by feature_057 + feature_035 + feature_011 + feature_006; the rest add limited incremental information.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The composite model's AUC is comparable across racial subgroups (no group with AUC < 0.55 or > 0.65), consistent with the earlier finding that key effects do not interact with race.

analyses.code: compute AUC within each level of feature_005
analyses.result_summary: AUC by race: white 0.597, asian 0.600, black 0.606, hispanic 0.596, other 0.584. Range only 0.022. Hypothesis 34 SUPPORTED — model performance is approximately equitable across the racial groups in this cohort.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the most favorable subgroup (feature_057=0), there is NO residual race × response association — i.e., even in the easiest-to-treat stratum, race does not independently predict response.

analyses.code: chi2_contingency(crosstab(feature_005, objective_response)) on df[df['feature_057']==0]
analyses.result_summary: Within feature_057=0 (n=17592): white 21.74%, hispanic 20.94%, black 20.46%, asian 20.37%, other 16.99%. Chi-square p=0.138 — NOT significant. The largest crude gap (white vs other = 4.8 pp) is driven by the small 'other' subgroup (n=359). Hypothesis 35 SUPPORTED. Combined with iteration 24, the analysis suggests no systematic disparity in predicted or observed response by race after accounting for the feature_057 strata, although the small 'other' category warrants caution due to limited sample size.
